articles.title: Introducing OpenAI Academy for News Organizations
articles.summary: OpenAI has launched the OpenAI Academy for News Organizations, a program designed to help newsrooms effectively utilize AI tools and mitigate potential risks. The academy aims to provide journalists and news organizations with the knowledge and skills needed to harness AI's potential while maintaining journalistic integrity. By doing so, OpenAI seeks to promote responsible AI adoption in the news industry and support high-quality journalism.

articles.title: Developers can now submit apps to ChatGPT
articles.summary: Developers can now submit their own apps to ChatGPT, enabling the AI model to interact with external services and expand its capabilities. This integration allows ChatGPT to access a broader range of functionalities, enhancing its user experience and potential applications. By opening up to third-party apps, ChatGPT is poised to become an even more powerful and versatile tool.

articles.title: Evaluating AI’s ability to perform scientific research tasks
articles.summary: Researchers are exploring AI's potential to assist with scientific research tasks, such as data analysis and hypothesis generation. A new study evaluates the capabilities and limitations of AI in performing these tasks, paving the way for increased efficiency and innovation in scientific discovery. By leveraging AI, scientists may be able to accelerate breakthroughs and uncover new insights in various fields.

articles.title: Measuring AI’s capability to accelerate biological research
articles.summary: Researchers are exploring the potential of AI to accelerate biological research, leveraging its capability to analyze vast amounts of data and identify patterns. This integration of AI in biology could lead to breakthroughs in disease diagnosis, treatment, and drug discovery. By automating routine tasks and providing insights, AI is poised to revolutionize the field of biological research and improve human health outcomes.

articles.title: The new ChatGPT Images is here
articles.summary: ChatGPT has unveiled its latest feature, ChatGPT Images, which enables users to generate images based on text prompts, revolutionizing the way we interact with AI. This innovation combines the power of language models with image generation capabilities, opening up new possibilities for creative applications. With ChatGPT Images, users can now bring their ideas to life with unprecedented ease and accuracy.

articles.title: Gemini 3 Flash: frontier intelligence built for speed
articles.summary: Gemini 3 Flash is a new AI model designed for speed and efficiency, aiming to revolutionize the field of frontier intelligence. This cutting-edge technology promises to deliver faster and more accurate results, making it a game-changer for various applications. With its enhanced capabilities, Gemini 3 Flash is poised to transform the way we approach complex problem-solving and data analysis.

articles.title: Improved Gemini audio models for powerful voice experiences
articles.summary: Google has upgraded its Gemini audio models, enabling more accurate and efficient voice experiences. This innovation allows for better speech recognition, music and audio processing, and overall improved performance in voice-activated applications. The enhanced models are set to power a new generation of voice-enabled devices and services.

articles.title: Deepening our partnership with the UK AI Security Institute
articles.summary: A leading tech company has announced a partnership with the UK AI Security Institute to advance AI security research and development. This collaboration aims to improve the security and resilience of AI systems, addressing growing concerns around AI-related threats. The partnership is expected to drive innovation and foster a safer AI ecosystem.

articles.title: Strengthening our partnership with the UK government to support prosperity and security in the AI era
articles.summary: The UK government has partnered with a tech company to harness AI for national prosperity and security, driving innovation and growth. This collaboration aims to leverage AI to address complex challenges and create new opportunities. By working together, they will foster a secure and thriving AI ecosystem that benefits both the public and private sectors.

articles.title: FACTS Benchmark Suite: Systematically evaluating the factuality of large language models
articles.summary: Researchers have introduced the FACTS Benchmark Suite, a comprehensive evaluation tool to assess the factuality of large language models, helping to identify and mitigate the spread of misinformation. This suite systematically tests a model's ability to distinguish between factual and non-factual information, providing a more accurate measure of its reliability. By using FACTS, developers can refine their models to produce more accurate and trustworthy outputs.

articles.title: The Open Evaluation Standard: Benchmarking NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator
articles.summary: NVIDIA has introduced the Open Evaluation Standard, a benchmarking framework that assesses the performance of AI models like the Nemotron 3 Nano using the NeMo Evaluator tool. This standard enables developers to compare and optimize their models more effectively, driving innovation in AI research and development. By providing a unified evaluation framework, NVIDIA aims to accelerate progress in the field of artificial intelligence.

articles.title: CUGA on Hugging Face: Democratizing Configurable AI Agents
articles.summary: Hugging Face has introduced CUGA, a platform that democratizes access to configurable AI agents, allowing users to easily create and customize their own AI models. This innovation enables a broader range of developers to build and deploy AI solutions, regardless of their expertise level. By making AI more accessible, CUGA has the potential to accelerate AI adoption and innovation across various industries.

articles.title: New in llama.cpp: Model Management
articles.summary: The latest update to llama.cpp introduces model management, allowing users to easily switch between different AI models and manage their configurations. This innovation enhances the flexibility and customizability of the llama.cpp framework, making it more accessible to developers and researchers. With model management, users can now experiment with various models and optimize their performance for specific applications.

articles.title: Codex is Open Sourcing AI models
articles.summary: Codex, a leading AI developer, is open-sourcing its AI models, making them freely available for use and modification by the public. This move is expected to accelerate innovation and collaboration in the AI community, driving advancements in areas like natural language processing and machine learning. By open-sourcing its models, Codex aims to democratize access to AI technology and foster a more transparent and inclusive development process.

articles.title: Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker: Cost-efficient Frontier Multimodal Performance
articles.summary: The Apriel-1.6-15b-Thinker model has achieved a breakthrough in multimodal performance, offering a cost-efficient solution for AI applications. This innovation enables more efficient processing of various data types, such as text, images, and audio, making it a significant advancement in the field of artificial intelligence. By optimizing performance while reducing costs, the Apriel model has the potential to expand AI accessibility across industries.

articles.title: Online Textbook for Braid groups and knots and tangles
articles.summary: A new online textbook on Braid groups and knots and tangles has been made available, providing a comprehensive resource for mathematicians and researchers. This free online textbook offers an in-depth exploration of the subject, covering topics such as knot invariants and braid group representations. The open-access nature of the textbook makes it easily accessible to anyone interested in learning about these complex mathematical concepts.

articles.title: Creating apps like Signal could be 'hostile activity' claims UK watchdog
articles.summary: The UK's Investigatory Powers Commissioner, a government watchdog, has sparked controversy by suggesting that creating encrypted messaging apps like Signal could be considered "hostile activity" due to their potential to hinder law enforcement efforts. This claim has raised concerns about the balance between national security and individual privacy rights. The statement may have significant implications for the development and use of secure communication tools in the UK.

articles.title: We Let AI Run Our Office Vending Machine. It Lost Hundreds of Dollars
articles.summary: An experiment where AI was used to manage an office vending machine resulted in significant losses, with the machine dispensing hundreds of dollars' worth of free snacks due to poor decision-making. The AI's lack of human intuition and common sense led to a series of costly mistakes, highlighting the limitations of relying solely on automation. This failed experiment serves as a cautionary tale for businesses considering AI implementation in everyday operations.

articles.title: AWS CEO says replacing junior devs with AI is 'one of the dumbest ideas'
articles.summary: AWS CEO Adam Selipsky has dismissed the idea of replacing junior developers with AI as "one of the dumbest ideas", emphasizing the importance of human talent and skills in the tech industry. He argues that while AI can augment developer tasks, it cannot replicate the creativity and problem-solving abilities of human developers. This stance highlights the value of human innovation and collaboration in driving technological advancements.

articles.title: A Safer Container Ecosystem with Docker: Free Docker Hardened Images
articles.summary: Docker has introduced free hardened images to enhance the security of its container ecosystem, providing users with a more secure foundation for their applications. These hardened images are designed to reduce the risk of common vulnerabilities and exposures, making it easier for developers to build and deploy secure containers. By offering these images for free, Docker aims to promote a safer and more trustworthy container environment.
